Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Verilog to Factorio Compiler and Simulator (Working RISC-V CPU) 101 upvotes, 11 comments
  2. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 416 upvotes, 71 comments
  3. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 1178 upvotes, 225 comments
  4. OpenBSD on Motorola 88000 Processors 111 upvotes, 15 comments
  5. The ANSI art "telecomics" of the 1992 election 65 upvotes, 7 comments
  6. CSS is DOOMed 402 upvotes, 94 comments
  7. Linux is an interpreter 218 upvotes, 52 comments
  8. Monado became the foundation for OpenXR runtimes 23 upvotes, 3 comments
  9. I decompiled the White House's new app 561 upvotes, 202 comments
  10. OpenCiv1 – open-source rewrite of Civ1 167 upvotes, 52 comments
  11. Lat.md: Agent Lattice: a knowledge graph for your codebase, written in Markdown 49 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Show HN: Create a full language server in Go with 3.17 spec support 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. Overestimation of microplastics potentially caused by scientists' gloves 183 upvotes, 71 comments
  14. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 87 upvotes, 31 comments
  15. Technology: The (nearly) perfect USB cable tester does exist 135 upvotes, 59 comments
  16. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 85 upvotes, 36 comments
  17. A laser-based process that enables adhesive-free paper packaging 94 upvotes, 41 comments
  18. Siclair Microvision (1977) 25 upvotes, 7 comments
  19. Show HN: Public transit systems as data – lines, stations, railcars, and history 27 upvotes, 9 comments
  20. What if AI doesn't need more RAM but better math? 89 upvotes, 50 comments
  21. Alzheimer's disease mortality among taxi and ambulance drivers (2024) 162 upvotes, 103 comments
  22. Further human + AI + proof assistant work on Knuth's "Claude Cycles" problem 228 upvotes, 155 comments
  23. Nonfiction Publishing, Under Threat, Is More Important 22 upvotes, 9 comments
  24. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 692 upvotes, 547 comments
  25. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 87 upvotes, 57 comments
  26. The Cloud: The dystopian book that changed Germany (2022) 28 upvotes, 15 comments
  27. TSA lines are so out of control that travelers are hiring line-sitters 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  28. The Failure of the Thermodynamics of Computation(2010) 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. Android’s new sideload settings will carry over to new devices 121 upvotes, 161 comments
  30. InpharmD (YC W21) Is Hiring – Senior Ruby on Rails Developer 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 10:00pm, 29 March 2026. Source here.
